qa_pairs.question: If a project needs 150 core-years on the Rorqual cluster with different memory requirements, how should the CPU resources be requested?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need 150 core-years on Rorqual with different memory requirements, such as 100 cores with 16 GB per core (resulting in 400 core-equivalent-years) and 50 cores with 4 GB per core (50 core-equivalent-years), you should make two separate CPU requests on Rorqual to account for the different memory requirements. An alternative acceptable option would be to average out the total memory per core needed and just make one request for 150 cores with 12 GB per core.

qa_pairs.question: Why is a CPU request for 150 core-years on a single cluster, split by different memory requirements, considered an acceptable RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A CPU request for 150 core-years on a single cluster, split by different memory requirements (e.g., 100 cores with 16 GB/core and 50 cores with 4 GB/core), is acceptable because the total core-equivalent-years needed on that single cluster (450 core-equivalent-years in this example) is greater than the minimum CPU amount specified in the table (200 core-years).
